dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires identification of established trading range edges (support/resistance). Used Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower) as proxy for range boundaries over 20-period lookback. Price consolidation near edge modeled using price_pos_20 (< 0.25 for lower edge, > 0.75 for upper edge). Consolidation tightness captured via bb_bandwidth threshold. Breakout confirmation requires close beyond range boundary plus volume above 20-period historical average. Exit conditions reverse trend on return to opposite range boundary.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires identification of established trading range edges (support/resistance). Used Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower) as proxy for range boundaries over 20-period lookback. Price consolidation near edge modeled using price_pos_20 (< 0.25 for lower edge, > 0.75 for upper edge). Consolidation tightness captured via bb_bandwidth threshold. Breakout confirmation requires close beyond range boundary plus volume above 20-period historical average. Exit conditions reverse trend on return to opposite range boundary.

source_rule_text: A trader should anticipate a breakout when price consolidates tightly near one edge of a trading range, as this indicates pressure building against that boundary rather than equilibrium.
